summary: At a World Cup for the first time since 1974 (as Zaire), reaching it the hard way — a 1-0 extra-time playoff win over Jamaica. Under Sébastien Desabre they maximise pace and physicality, and qualified with a stingy defence (seven wins in 10, just six goals conceded) — a world away from Zaire's 14-conceded group exit in '74.
style: A 4-3-3 that, like Portugal, creates a five-on-four in attack — full-backs pushed ahead, attackers bunched close — and can be direct or play quick passing sequences from the back to free midfielders (a triangle vs Senegal sprung a switch to Bongonda, then an underlapping Wan-Bissaka and a Bakambu rebound). Out of possession, usually a passive, deep 4-4-2, sometimes a 4-1-4-1.

counterReliance.note: Pace-and-physicality transitions off a deep central block

strengths: Stingy defence — only six conceded in qualifying
Pace and physicality on the break, with attackers bunched to spring switches
Quick passing sequences from the back to find midfielders in space
weaknesses: The 4-1-4-1 can be opened by players between the lines (too much for the lone holder)
Direct approach can leave them exposed on the counter themselves

keyPlayers.name: Noah Sadiki
keyPlayers.note: 21-year-old all-action engine after a strong PL debut season — snaps into tackles, roams to win it back, and a balanced carrier who keeps possession ticking

keyPlayers.name: Cédric Bakambu
keyPlayers.note: One goal from equalling the all-time record (22) — the finisher of the transitions

keyPlayers.name: Yoane Wissa
keyPlayers.note: Premier League goal threat to share the scoring load

tactics: Defend a compact central block, break with pace and physicality into the five-on-four, and mix directness with sharp build-up passing. Congesting the space between the lines is the key to keeping technical teams (Portugal, Colombia) out.
notes: First World Cup in 52 years; reached it via the inter-confederation playoff.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group K (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
